lever: row-chunked disjoint-write dense i64 inner join output fill: replace per-column workers (threads = column count, full probe walk per column) with up to 16 output-size-balanced row chunks, each filling its disjoint split_at_mut slice of EVERY column in one probe walk
scenario: inner_join 100000x3 (512 distinct keys -> 19.5M output rows), 3 iters (perf_profile, release-perf, 64-core host)
mechanism: 3-4 column-worker threads -> 16 chunk workers; one bucket-probe walk per chunk instead of per column; page faults on the ~470MB/iter freshly zero-allocated output distributed across all workers (process System time was 5x User time before); right-side bucket value tapes (muis1) built once and shared read-only

isomorphism.ordering_preserved: yes - chunks partition the left probe order; concatenated chunk output = single-threaded walk output, byte for byte
isomorphism.floating_point: n/a - i64 only; all non-i64 columns still reject the fused path upstream
isomorphism.fallbacks: serial arm unchanged; null/mixed/sorted/indicator/validate paths untouched; gate widened from (2..=8 columns) to (>=1 column) which only changes scheduling, not output

golden_sha256.note: bead-quoted shas (494106../102690..) date from an older harness capture shape; gate enforced as before==after on current main

isomorphism.sink_check: sink=58594080 identical before/after (19531360 output rows x 3 iters)

test_gates.fp-join --lib --tests: 101 passed, 0 failed (cfg(test) MIN_VALUES=1 forces the parallel chunked path under test)
